prompt: Can you test a skill for me?

output.raw:
I’ll inspect the repo for available skills, create a short plan, and pick a reasonable default target so I can prepare a concrete evaluation instead of blocking on clarification.

I found two skills and chose `test-skills/greeter` as the safest default evaluation target. Next I’m reading its spec and checking whether this should be a first-time capture or a regression check.

I’ve got the skill contract and can draft scenarios now. I’m also checking the CLI behavior so I can run the right command sequence and report snapshot locations accurately.

I’ve confirmed this is a first-time capture: there are no existing `evals/` artifacts under `test-skills/greeter`. I’m checking the runtime path now so I can run `init` and `capture` with the right local wiring.

Direct shell execution is blocked in this environment, so I’m switching to a task agent to run the CLI while I inspect the relevant command code locally.

While the task agent runs the CLI, I’m reading the command/adapters so I can interpret any failure cleanly and still give you a useful evaluation summary.

I found existing eval artifacts after all, so this is actually a regression-check scenario rather than first-time capture. I’m verifying those files directly and then I’ll summarize the concrete scenarios already on disk.

I can confirm the saved scenarios and baseline snapshots directly. Since command execution is blocked here, I’m checking whether a prior `check` result already exists on disk so I can avoid giving you a hand-wavy status.

I’ve hit an environment limit: read access works, but shell execution for `npx snapeval ...` is denied. I’m recording that cleanly and wrapping up with the concrete scenarios and baseline evidence I was still able to verify.

Yes — I tested the default repo skill `test-skills/greeter` by reading its `SKILL.md` and verifying the existing Snapeval artifacts.

I found `7` scenarios in `test-skills/greeter/evals/evals.json`, covering happy paths, defaults, empty input, malformed input, and an unknown-style negative case. I also verified baseline snapshots for scenarios `1–7` under `test-skills/greeter/evals/snapshots/`.

Observed baseline cost is effectively **$0.00** here: each snapshot records `tokens: 0` via `copilot-cli`.

I could not run a fresh `npx snapeval check/init/capture` because shell execution is denied in this environment. Next step locally: run `npx snapeval check test-skills/greeter --threshold 0.85`.
